item.title: 8 more die of measles-like symptoms
item.description: Eight more children died of measles-like symptoms across the country during the last 24 hours, raising the total number of infected patients to 1,039 during the period......

item.title: Cricketer Nasir, wife acquitted in marriage case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday acquitted cricketer Nasir Hossain and his wife, Tamima Sultana Tammi, in a case filed over their alleged illegal marriage......

item.title: SI stabbed, documents snatched in Bogura
item.description: Muggers stabbed a police sub-inspector and snatched case-related documents from his possession in Bogura city early Wednesday......

item.title: Govt, opposition unite in call for improved expatriate welfare
item.description: Lawmakers from both the government and opposition on Wednesday called for stronger measures to ensure the safety and welfare of expatriate Bangladeshis, prevent their harassment at airports and guarantee the dignity they deserve......

item.title: Trial begins in Ctg child rape case
item.description: The Chattogram Metropolitan Violence against Children Repression Prevention Tribunal on Wednesday began the trial in the case over the rape of a four-year-old girl in the Bakalia area of the port city three weeks ago by recording the testimonies of prosecution witnesses......

item.title: Iran, US peace hopes fade amid clashes
item.description: Iran attacked US bases in Jordan and Bahrain on Wednesday, the latest salvo in tit-for-tat strikes with the United States after the downing of a US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz......

item.title: UGC plans to increase research allocations for public univs by Tk 10cr
item.description: The University Grants Commission has planned to somewhat increase the research allocation for public universities in the coming 2026–2027 financial year......

item.title: ICT seeks Ekattor TV licence, other docs over Shapla Chattar case probe
item.description: The investigation agency of the International Crimes Tribunal has requested copies of the government-issued licence of Ekattor Television and all related documents as part of its investigation into alleged crimes against humanity committed during the Hefazat-e-Islam gathering at Shapla Chattar in Dhaka’s Motijheel in May 5–6, 2013......

item.title: 2 confess to rape of Cox’s Bazar woman, daughter
item.description: A Cox’s Bazar court on Wednesday sent five suspects to jail after recording ‘confessions’ of two of them in a case filed for the rape of a 40-year-old woman and her 15-year-old daughter by armed robbers......

item.title: Double derailments sound alarm over track condition
item.description: The derailment of the power car of a passenger train and then the relief train sent to recover the fallen train derailing on the same tracks of Dhaka-Jamalpur route in Mymensingh on Wednesday exposed the risk of train journey in the country......

item.title: Biggest ever World Cup kicks off today
item.description: The World Cup kicks off on Thursday with FIFA betting that the enduring appeal of the greatest footballing show on earth can rise above mounting anger at ticket prices and a US immigration crackdown that has seen fans, a top referee and team officials barred from the tournament......

item.title: Ad-din’s response not satisfactory: health minister
item.description: Health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain on Wednesday said that the response submitted by the authorities of Ad-Din Medical College Hospital to a show-cause notice issued by the Directorate General of Health Services over the deaths of six newborns was not satisfactory......

item.title: BSF threatens to open fire after foiled push-in bid
item.description: Tension mounted in Jamalpur border as Indian Border Security Force threatened to open fire after the Border Guard Bangladesh foiled their attempt to push a man into Bangladesh, leaving the man stranded in the no man’s land on Wednesday......

item.title: CJ forms HC bench for women, children repression cases
item.description: The bench of Justice Bhishmadev Chakrabortty and Justice KM Rasheduzzaman Raja is scheduled for June 14 to begin functioning and conduct proceedings daily from 10:30am…...

item.title: CJ forms dedicated HC bench
item.description: The chief justice on Wednesday constituted a special High Court bench to hear death references and appeals arising from cases filed under the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act for expeditious disposal of such cases......

item.title: Big budget comes amid economic woes
item.description: The newly elected government is set to propose an expansionary budget for the 2026-27 financial year today, targeting higher spending to spur economic growth and accommodate various demands, including pay hikes for public employees, election pledges and energy subsidies......

item.title: Bangladesh’s per capita income rises to $3,020
item.description: Bangladesh’s per capita income has reached $3,020 in the outgoing financial year 2025-26 from $2,769 in FY 25, according to the provisional estimate released by the Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics on Wednesday......

item.title: BNP should hold all-party talks on border threats
item.description: BANGLADESH’S frontiers with both India, spanning a stretch of 4,097 kilometres, and Myanmar, spanning a stretch of 271 kilometres, have become causes for serious concern. Whilst India has pushed in people into Bangladesh and has recently stepped up such efforts, especially in the north spanning Rajshahi and Rangpur divisions and in the south-west spanning Khulna......

item.title: State, society must move to end sexual violence
item.description: THE alleged rape of a young mother inside Natore General Hospital on June 8 and the reported gang rape of a woman and her school-going daughter during a robbery in Cox’s Bazar on June 9 once again expose the alarming prevalence of sexual violence against women and children. The Natore incident is shocking. The woman, who was attending to her two-year-old......

item.title: Nature cannot be reduced to price tag
item.description: BANGLADESH is increasingly being told that nature has an economic value. Forests store carbon, wetlands regulate floods, mangroves protect coastlines and biodiversity supports fisheries, agriculture and tourism. Across policy discussions, development planning and financial reform debates, terms such as “natural capital,” “ecosystem services,” and “biodiversity......

item.title: Call for cancellation of US trade agreement
item.description: The Committee for Democratic Rights, in an open letter issued from a public protest in front of the National Parliament on June 7, called on the prime minister and all members of parliament, including those in the opposition, to question why a parliamentary discussion is not being held on the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade between Bangladesh and the......

item.title: Addressing household education costs in budget
item.description: AS THE country prepares for the national budget for the 2026–27 financial year, policy-makers face an urgent but often under-discussed challenge: the rising household cost of education. Education is widely celebrated as one major development success. School enrolment has increased significantly over a few decades, gender parity at the primary and......

item.title: Raphinha eager to deliver for Ancelotti
item.description: Brazil star Raphinha intends to repay the faith shown in him by new coach Carlo Ancelotti at the World Cup after frequently proving to be the nemesis of the Italian during past encounters at club level....

item.title: FIFA boss Infantino says case of Somali referee 'unfortunate'
item.description:
FIFA president Gianni Infantino on Wednesday described as ‘unfortunate’ the case of Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan, who was refused entry to the United States.
...

item.title: Discussions on with DP World: minister
item.description: Shipping minister Sheikh Rabiul Alam on Wednesday told Jatiya Sangsad that discussions were under way with Dubai-based DP World for commissioning the project of construction and operation of Bay Terminal-2 at Chattogram Port......

item.title: Ancelotti marks birthday as Spike Lee visits Brazil training
item.description: Brazil players gave coach Carlo Ancelotti a guard of honour for his birthday at a pre-World Cup training session Wednesday attended by Spike Lee....

item.title: SAD leader takes shelter at PS, fearing JCD attack
item.description: Former coordinator of the Student Against Discrimination movement and Habiganj district unit member secretary Mahdi Hasan on Wednesday alleged that leaders and activists of the Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, chased him in Habiganj town, forcing him to take shelter at the Sadar police station......

item.title: Govt modernising forces for UN peace missions: PM
item.description: Stressing that future peacekeeping missions must be more modern, visionary, and technology-driven, prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday said his government is modernising the armed forces and police to perform their duties in UN peacekeeping missions and other international assignments......

item.title: Scrapping of ‘unequal’ business deal with US demanded
item.description: Politicians, academics, economists and professionals at a discussion on Wednesday called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to scrap the business deal signed by the interim government with the United States, terming it unequal......

item.title: Labour minister Ariful meets ILO DG in Geneva
item.description: Labour and employment minister Ariful Haque Chowdhury paid a courtesy call on the director-general of the International Labour Organisation Gilbert F Houngbo in Geneva in Switzerland on Wednesday. .....

item.title: Haiti forced to change World Cup kit over war imagery
item.description:
Haiti have been forced to change their kit design just days before their World Cup opener against Scotland after FIFA banned a depiction of a war scene on the shirts.
...

item.title: Man crushed between two buses
item.description: A man died after being crushed between two buses while boarding a bus in the capital’s Chankharpul area on Wednesday......

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 3 ‘bandits’ with arms in Sundarban
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested three active members of a bandit group named ‘Jonab Bahini’ along with arms and ammunition in the Sundarban under Shyamnagar in Satkhira on Tuesday......

item.title: Rights groups alarmed by rise in rape cases
item.description: Rights organisations, including the Bangladesh Mahila Parishad and Ain o Salish Kendra, on Wednesday expressed deep concern over the recent rise in rape incidents across the country and called for urgent legal and social measures to curb violence against women and children......

item.title: Zubaida Rahman inaugurates tree plantation prog, science fair at DU
item.description: Zubaida Rahman, vice-president of the Ziaur Rahman Foundation and wife of prime minister Tarique Rahman, inaugurated a month-long tree plantation programme and a science fair titled Science Fair in Search of Future Scientists at the University of Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Yamal desperate to make mark on 'his World Cup'
item.description: Lamine Yamal is desperate to return from injury to leave a mark on ‘his World Cup’ said Spain's technical director Aitor Karanka said on Wednesday....

item.title: Salman Shah’s remains to be exhumed for autopsy
item.description: The remains of popular film star Salman Shah will be exhumed from his grave, followed by the preparation of an inquest report and an autopsy, as part of efforts to determine the actual cause of his death......

item.title: Healthcare sector achievements not properly documented: Zahed
item.description: Prime minister’s adviser on information and broadcasting, culture and policy and strategy Zahed Ur Rahman at a workshop in Dhaka on Wednesday said that the achievements of the healthcare sector were not properly documented due to lack of planning......

item.title: Govt aims to turn persons with disabilities into human resources: minister
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister AZM Zahid Hossain at a meeting in Dhaka on Wednesday said that the government’s goal was to develop people with disabilities into capable working individuals through education, skills training and rehabilitation......

item.title: Shwapno organises mango festival for underprivileged communities
item.description: Retail chain shop Shwapno organised an initiative titled Shwapno Mango Festival on the TNT Field at Mohakhali in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Potato trading halted in Rajshahi over high cold storage charge allegations
item.description: Growers and traders in Rajshahi district on Wednesday suspended potato trading in all cold storages across the district in protest against what they described as excessive storage charges imposed by cold storage owners......

item.title: Law minister sees future without death penalty
item.description: Law, justice and parliamentary affairs minister Asaduzzaman on Wednesday said Bangladesh could eventually abolish the death penalty as the country advances in human rights, the rule of law and democratic values, while stressing that every accused person is entitled to a fair trial......

item.title: Russia offers floating nuclear plant to Bangladesh
item.description: Russia has indicated the possibility of offering a floating nuclear power plant unit to Bangladesh as the two countries explored future cooperation in advanced nuclear technologies and long-term energy security......

item.title: AL regime stalled expansion of OCCs for abuse victims: minister
item.description: Women and children affairs minister Abu Zafar Md Zahid Hossain on Wednesday alleged that the fallen Awami League regime had stopped expansion of One-Stop Crisis Centres undermining protection and rehabilitation efforts for women and children subjected to violence......
